summary: Large mechanical nomenclature cleanup for the game scene system. The PR replaces the repository's ambiguous Major/Minor scene terminology with GameMode/GameScene terminology across gm headers, implementations, scene tables, symbols, and table-typer tooling. It matters because the old Major/Minor naming had become community shorthand but was considered ambiguous; this made mode-vs-scene routing fields, enum names, table names, and function names more semantically explicit.
what_changed: Renamed MajorSceneKind to GameModeKind and changed enum constants from MJ_* to GM_* in src/melee/gm/forward.h and many call sites.
Renamed MinorSceneKind to GameSceneKind and changed enum constants from MN_* to GS_*.
Renamed core scene structs and typedefs: MajorScene to GameMode, MinorScene to GameScene, MinorSceneHandler to GameSceneHandler, and MinorSceneInfo to GameSceneInfo.
Renamed scene setter APIs from gm_SetSceneMinor to gm_SetScene and gm_SetScenePendingMinor to gm_SetPendingScene.

Renamed GameState routing field vocabulary from nums.curr_minor, prev_minor, pending_minor, curr_major, prev_major, and pending_major to routing.curr_scene, prev_scene, pending_scene, curr_mode, prev_mode, and pending_mode.
Renamed data symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from *_MinorScenes to *_Scenes while preserving addresses, sizes, scopes, and object metadata.
Updated many scene table definitions across gm files from MinorScene arrays to GameScene arrays and from *_MinorScenes names to *_Scenes names.
Updated helper names in gm_1A3F.c such as matchMinor to nextScene and getScene/findSceneMatching-style naming toward scene/mode terminology.
Updated table-typer tooling references so generated or inferred table types track the new GameMode/GameScene nomenclature.
smart_moves: The cleanup was applied broadly rather than partially: headers, implementations, scene tables, symbol names, and tooling were kept in sync, reducing future mixed Major/Minor vs GameMode/GameScene drift.
The PR preserved enum numeric values and comments while only changing type and enumerator names, keeping behavior and table layout stable.
Symbol renames retained exact addresses and sizes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, which is important for decompilation symbol continuity.
The new terms separate high-level game modes from per-mode scenes more directly than Major/Minor, making routing code like curr_mode and curr_scene easier to read.
Renaming internal state grouping from nums to routing made the state fields describe their purpose instead of their representation.
decomp_lessons: When replacing widely used terminology, update forward declarations, struct typedefs, enum names, constants, function prototypes, definitions, symbols, and tooling in the same PR to avoid a half-renamed decomp surface.
For pure nomenclature changes, preserve enum order, table sentinel values, field order, addresses, and symbol metadata; this keeps the change low-risk and reviewable.
Avoid ambiguous hierarchy names when the code really models different concepts: use GameMode for the outer mode and GameScene for the routed scene entries.
Config symbol names should follow source naming changes so searches and generated references do not continue pointing at stale terminology.
Tooling that emits or reasons about table types must be renamed alongside source types, otherwise future table work may reintroduce old names.
naming_conventions: Use GameModeKind with GM_* constants for the former MajorSceneKind/MJ_* values.
Use GameSceneKind with GS_* constants for the former MinorSceneKind/MN_* values.
Use GameMode, GameScene, GameSceneHandler, and GameSceneInfo instead of MajorScene, MinorScene, MinorSceneHandler, and MinorSceneInfo.
Use gm_SetScene and gm_SetPendingScene instead of gm_SetSceneMinor and gm_SetScenePendingMinor.
Use mode-oriented parameter names such as mode, mode_kind, and pending_mode instead of major_scene, major_kind, and pending_major.
Use scene-oriented parameter and field names such as scene, curr_scene, prev_scene, and pending_scene instead of minor_scene, curr_minor, prev_minor, and pending_minor.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-specific matching tactic was documented in the PR text or comments.
The diff appears to be a behavior-preserving mechanical rename: added/deleted line counts are nearly equal and symbol entries keep identical addresses and sizes.
For future matching-safe cleanups, follow this pattern of changing names without changing enum numeric values, table order, or struct layout.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.body: LGTM. Like I said before, I think the biggest thing is the community has settled on the Major/Minor thing and it'll just be another difference that'll have to be reconciled eventually.
review_feedback.lesson: Reviewer accepted the cleanup but noted ecosystem cost: changing established community terminology can create another naming difference that future contributors may need to reconcile.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining uses of MajorScene or MinorScene terminology after PR 2517.
Check whether community docs or wiki pages still use Major/Minor scene names and need aliases to GameMode/GameScene.
Review table-typer output for GameMode/GameScene naming consistency.
Search for gm_SetSceneMinor or gm_SetScenePendingMinor in downstream branches before rebasing onto this cleanup.
